description: Retuned weighted scalarization to prioritize activity and toxicity, with secondary stability and lighter developability penalty.
Tried pure four-endpoint rank-product aggregation; it improved only the rank-product oracle and regressed from the current best mean.
Tried a soft threshold-gated multiplicative policy; it boosted the threshold-gate oracle but regressed the mean and rank-product diagnostics.
Tried Pareto-front layering with a linear tie-break; it matched the mean but added complexity and underperformed the current best on NDCG.
Increase activity weight from 0.45 to 0.50 while holding other Prompt 3 weights fixed.
Decrease activity weight from 0.45 to 0.40 while holding other Prompt 3 weights fixed.
Increase toxicity penalty weight from 0.40 to 0.45 while holding other Prompt 3 weights fixed.
Decrease toxicity penalty weight from 0.40 to 0.35 while holding other Prompt 3 weights fixed.
Increase stability weight from 0.25 to 0.30 while holding other Prompt 3 weights fixed.
Decrease stability weight from 0.25 to 0.20 while holding other Prompt 3 weights fixed.
Increase developability penalty weight from 0.15 to 0.20 while holding other Prompt 3 weights fixed.
Decrease developability penalty weight from 0.15 to 0.10 while holding other Prompt 3 weights fixed.
Cap normalized developability penalty at 0.60 before applying the Prompt 3 weight to avoid over-penalizing already bad sequences.
Use a mild nonlinear toxicity penalty nt^1.5 with the Prompt 3 weights to punish highly toxic candidates slightly more strongly.
